verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 is a trans-Golgi network resident protein with a TM domain that anchors it to intracellular endomembranes (TGN, early/recycling endosomes). While it undergoes vesicular trafficking as part of secretory cycling, the canonical and steady-state compartment is intracellular. No documented PM residence, shedding, surface therapeutic targeting, cancer ecto-display, cell-death externalization, or germline/activation-restricted surface pool. The TM domain anchors the protein to the TGN, not the plasma membrane.
